supply_chain.dependencies: Single-source or limited-source suppliers for read/write heads, substrates for recording media, ASICs, spindle motors, printed circuit boards, suspension assemblies, NAND flash memory, and rare earth elements primarily sourced from China
supply_chain.risks: Shortages or delays in critical components, cost increases, reliance on single-source suppliers, geographically concentrated supplier base vulnerable to regional disruptions, rare earth element export restrictions from China, long lead times for custom manufacturing equipment

regulatory_risks: Export control laws and regulations (Export Administration Regulations - EAR)
U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctions
BIS Settlement Agreement compliance requirements including audits and denial order suspension
EU Restriction of the Use of Certain Hazardous Substances in Electrical and Electronic Equipment (RoHS)
EU REACH Directive (Registration, Evaluation, Authorization, and Restriction of Chemicals)
Data privacy, data protection and data security laws (state, federal, and international)
Environmental laws governing discharges, hazardous waste management, and contaminated site cleanup
Comprehensive Environmental Response Compensation and Liability Act (Superfund law)
Climate change regulations and sustainability requirements
Tax law changes including OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax
Trade policy changes including tariffs, quotas, and trade restrictions
Price controls and international trade regulations in jurisdictions like China, Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, and EU

sanction_risks: U.S. export restrictions to Huawei and other Chinese entities
Potential expansion of U.S. sanctions on advanced computing chips and technologies to China
Russia sanctions exposure due to Ukraine conflict
Retaliatory trade measures by foreign countries in response to U.S. tariffs
Rare earth element export restrictions or bans by Chinese government
Prohibition from selling to key customers due to export regulations
